thus may trying deflect criticism describing `` kubla khan '' `` psychological curiosity '' ( ) .
often times `` adapted ( simply adopted ) passages writers , little acknowledgement , sometimes context seems designed obfuscate literary motives reveal subterfuge '' ( ) .
coleridge likely created `` kubla khan '' , lack ethics certainly allowed alter `` true '' story saw fit .
preface coleridge claims `` awakening appeared distinct recollection whole , taking pen , ink paper , instantly eagerly wrote lines preserved '' ( ) .
given rhyme scheme stanza form , seems strange poem well written could composed asleep .
doubtful wrote , `` lacked application staying power ( ) , coleridge must worked poem .

b. g. galef , jr. part : little history humans profoundly social animals .
biological success species - numbers , worldwide distribution - depends small measure ability acquire behaviour socially : use , generations , improve upon one another 's responses environmental challenge .
members species , however , unique using behaviour others guide responses life 's demands .
like us , various insects , fishes , birds , mammals exploit behaviour conspecifics bias behavioural development adaptive directions .
humans , apparently , exceptional spontaneous use arbitrary signs symbols communicate fellows .
may well unusual intentionally teaching others behave ( barnett , ; ewer , ; galef & dalrymple , ) extent acquire motor skills observing imitating accomplished fellows ( davis , ; galef , b ; roberts , ; thorpe , ) .
however , recently evidence supporting even relatively simple statements differences processes supporting social learning members species become available .
many decades , processes used animals humans learn socially assumed identical .
begin gall ?

caveats six caveats order .
first , book deals almost ex clusively jurisdictions equal-pay-for-work-of-equal-value legislation place .

fifth caveat concerns 'class ' .
although working-class trade-union organizations clashed business-class associations shape pay-equity leg islation , sense class consideration .
framers pay-equity legislation tended ignore question ethnic racial differences , also class differences , favour exclusive emphasis gender differences rates pay .
little discussion value working-class labour compared work performed managers employers , class superiors workers .
retrospective critical examination equal pay work equal value , george ehring , legislative assistant ndp caucus ontario legislature , observed : 'what really need mechanism compares relative value work performed secretaries , childcare workers hair-dressers bosses . '
sixth caveat difficult discuss .
considerable ambiguity literature precise meanings 'equal pay ' , 'equal work ' , 'equal value ' , 'equivalent value ' , 'comparable worth ' , 'pay equity ' .
'equal work ' based comparisons jobs identical .
'similar work ' depends comparisons jobs similar substantially similar .
'equal value ' , use american term , 'comparable worth ' , based comparisons jobs may quite dissimilar equated terms composite effort , skill , responsibility , working conditions ( subdimensions four factors listed appendix b ) .
ambiguity use terms reflects part nature class feminist struggles business anti-feminists attempt restrict meaning pay equity equal pay identical similar work , labourites feminists attempt broaden term include equal pay work equal comparable value .
vancouver women 's resource centre raised question whether state used term 'pay equity ' - rather potent 'equal pay work equal value ' - co-opt defeat feminist drive truly equivalent wages women men .
one example might use 'pay equity ' ontario pay equity act , went effect jan. .
act debated amended , lobby groups sides issue widely interpreted mean equal pay work equal value .
although term 'equal value ' appears act , complete phrase 'equal pay work equal value ' , despite efforts ontario equal pay coalition inserted .

jobs deemed comparable skill , effort , responsibility working conditions apply . '
pay equity , hand , ' policy legislated provision requiring work performed women comparable value performed men establishment paid .
jobs evaluated according composite skill , effort , responsibility working conditions . '

m. morphology individual tephra grains consists three distinct types .
common form thin polygonal plates straight edges , often associated elongated three-sided prisms .
prisms often contain inclusions minerals gas .
type informally identified bubble-wall material .
second type thick , irregular somewhat elon gated grains large amounts gas mineral inclu sions .
material resembles comminuted pumice .
third type elongated cylindrical shapes oval cross section ( needles ) .
enclosures gas often present bubble trains elongated along long axis .

general definition expert system expert system computer applic ation solving problems would require extensive human expertise ( rolston ) .
perform task , expert system simulates reasoning process combining knowledge search techniques ( usually referred inferences ) .
rolston ( ) characterizes ideal expert system one includes following : ( ) extensive specific knowledge field interest ; ( ii ) application search techniques ; ( iii ) support heuristic analysis ; ( iv ) limited capacity infer `` new knowledge '' existing knowledge ; ( v ) symbolic processing ; ( vi ) ability explain reasoning .

rules vary strictly procedural ( well-defined invariant ) heuristic ( practices procedures valu able incapable proof gathered experience ) .

uses knowledge base modify expand contents working memory .
simple words , inference engine search mechanism .

heuristic algorithm power-network clustering un algorithme heuristique appliqu & eacute ; au probl & egrave ; de l'il & ocircumflex ; tage des r & eacute ; seaux de puissance hesham k. temraz victor h. quintana efficient heuristic algorithm solving cluster problems associated partitioning power networks presented paper .
algorithm divided two stages .
first stage creates initial partition based electrical distance system buses .
second stage involves interchanging pairs buses among various clusters initial partition .
first stage solves placement problem n connected buses r - dimensional euclidean space ; problem reduced finding r eigenvectors connectivity matrix , defined bus admittance matrix .
second stage based node interchange technique .
node interchange iterative heuristic method used improve initial partition .
method moves one bus time , one cluster initial partition another , attempt maximize total electrical distance clusters final partition .
applications proposed algorithm small medium-size power systems illustrated paper .

fast track failure : experts study perils work addiction success-oriented , term `` workaholic '' often positive term used describe fast-track baby boomers earned corporate reputations long hours tireless devotion detail .
carried bulging briefcases , worked late sacrificed weekends hope promotions , higher unclear word - higher profits employers .
psychologists say people , relentless pursuit on-the-job perfection actually results diminished productivity .
indeed , experts say eventually , workaholics lose sense control work lives .
occurs , person addicted work turn moody , become prone rages alienate family , friends colleagues .
said barbara killinger , toronto psychologist : `` worst , workaholics soulless - 's nothing inside , lost integrity . ''
according killinger , whose book workaholics : respectable addicts published key porter books april , workaholics men women addicted working , result , experience personality changes .
yet despite sometimes alarming consequences addiction , killinger says problem still widely misunderstood .
experts say increasing number people seeking treatment workaholism .
addiction takes hold slowly , killinger said co-workers spouses tend dismiss early symptoms workaholism - anxiety , irritability restlessness - temporary side effects overwork .
time , kenneth des roches , ottawa-based employment counsellor peat marwick stevenson & kellogg , management consulting firm , says employers sometimes make problem worse assuming someone workaholic attributes model employee .
giving workaholic responsibility prestige , says des roches , regularly counsels individuals suffering workaholism , employers unwittingly push workaholic far .
still , des roches says main problem obsession itself. ``
people get high work , becomes something vortex get caught , '' said. ``
long , need , n't want live without . ''
although word `` workaholism '' use years , psychologists say addiction existed much longer .
add problem recently gained greater recognition people largest segment north american population , ages , stage careers , according des roches , workaholism `` tends get people . ''

bart , , asked last name withheld , said stopping work completely able deal workaholism. ``
work way avoiding feelings , like booze drugs , '' said. ``
work gives feeling 're control . ''
killinger , studied worka holism eight years , says classic workaholic starts perfectionist desperately needs approval others .
time , says , working becomes compulsion , finally , single-minded pursuit self-glorification lead emotional problems personality changes .
striving power control , workaholics suffer chronic fatigue guilt , racked fears sometimes even become incapable distinguishing right wrong .
part , susan butt , psychologist university british columbia vancouver , says workaholics reach crisis stage begin lose control lives .
happens workaholics , says butt , `` lose sense well-being . ''
according killinger , workaholics capable lying , cheating , extreme cases , violence serves purposes .
increasingly bound work , yet feeling control , may become impatient children lash spouses. ``
, '' said killinger , `` realizing much damage done spouse . ''

many companies beginning realize obsessed work counterproductive , '' said .

none reached , commission , warranted , appoint conciliator try find settlement .
failing , tribunal established hear testimony public .

employer individual charged harassment could appeal commission 's decision courts .
cases , process take years appeals heard final ruling rendered .

gene therapy excites medical searchers , theory , offers way treat root cause herited disease , including many fatal illnesses currently effective treatment cure .
instead treating symptoms result genetic defect , gene therapy seeks correct problem source , way , provide lasting cure .
picture gene therapy offers new hope seemingly one-sided battle , inherited diseases known result defective single gene .
better-known ones include muscular dystro phy , cf , sickle-cell anaemia , tay-sachs disease , amyotrophic lateral scle rosis ( lou gehrig 's disease ) .
potential applications gene therapy go far beyond single-gene dis orders .
genetic defects also known suspected play role society 's common deadliest diseases , including many types cancer , alzheimer 's disease , haemophilia , diabetes .
many instances , un derlying genetic basis illness yet understood may involve complex interaction several genes .
processes become known , searchers hope gene therapy help correct conditions .
huge promise , human gene therapy thin hard clinical data show works .
scientists -year-old french anderson , led first government-approved human gene-therapy trial four years ago , theo rizing since late s. 's anderson sent article subject new england journal medicine , rejected heated debate .
take years jim wilson could successfully apply concept gene therapy first time ?

reality , gene ther apy anything simple .

second , must able deliver right genes safely right target cells , either bloodstream , liver , lung , brain .
must get transplanted genes work properly order correct biochemical breakdown causes disease .
finding gene enormously difficult .
basic units heredity , genes segments dna nu cleus cell contain instruc tions make proteins , basic build ing blocks life .

potent particles : canadian scientist makes key discovery fall , john simpson , professor physics ontario 's university guelph , carried experiment produced results could crucial scientists ' understanding universe - ultimate destiny .
simpson trying measure mass elementary particles called electron neutrinos , emitted atoms radioactive decay .
december year , simpson made discovery many scientists said probably erroneous : found neutrinos much higher mass scientists previously believed possible .
last november , scientists laboratory berkeley , calif. , disclosed duplicated simpson 's results .
physicists said , result , theorists may face prospect rethink widely held theories creation composition universe .
said gerald roy , nuclear physicist university alberta edmonton : `` theory correct , turn lot things upside-down physics world . ''
simpson said discovered follow-up experiments neutrinos two different masses , including masses kiloelectronvolts ( mass subatomic matter quoted units energy ) .
scientists previously said neutrinos little mass .
simpson made discovery help particle accelerator , device shoots atomic particles one-tenth speed light , mcmaster university hamilton .
simpson published findings new york city-based journal physical review letters april , , scientists canada , united states europe unable duplicate findings experiments .
well , simpson , -year-old native north bay , ont. , scholarship england 's oxford university , earned phd physics , said widespread skepticism discovery caused `` problems physics know . ''
added , `` people ashamed tell believe . ''
, experimental findings reported scientists u.s. department energy 's lawrence berkeley laboratory berkeley could change .

confirmation simpson 's discovery could lead major revision accepted theories nature , eventual fate , universe .
according body scientific thought known big bang theory , universe created cosmic explosion billion years ago .
supporters big bang theory say , ever since , matter universe , including stars planets , moving away starting point continue throughout eternity .
adherents rival theory contend expanding universe may reach point , total mass universe great enough , slow , stop begin contracting towards centre .
happens , theorists say , densely compacted mass centre universe eventually heat destroy exploding - set new universe motion .
recently , physicists calculated total mass universe sufficient cause stop expanding .
calculations based belief neutrinos virtually mass .
although simpson 's findings would apply one-third neutrinos , roy said heavy neutrinos exist , previous calculations would mean effect gravity would eventually cause universe contract .

though terms like light loss fall convenient , misleading .

darkroom workers understand dispersion .

key , technically speaking , inverse square law .

unhealthy buildings : experts say indoor air make people sick early , several federal civil servants working montreal office tower began complaining headaches , fatigue nausea .

said inspected building discovered automobile exhaust fumes underground parking garage penetrating offices owner building boarded underground ventilation system save money .
although source problem unusual , concerns air quality workplace becoming increasingly common .
indeed , almost , scientists , doctors businessmen countries met toronto last week six-day conference aimed improving air quality homes offices .
indoor air quality began emerge health issue late builders across north america europe started installing sealed windows , opened , energy conservation measure almost new office towers , hospitals schools .
experts attending toronto conference said air delivered ventilation systems frequently becomes contaminated pollutants inside outside building .
workers begin complaining headaches , fatigue ailments , suffering experts call `` sick-building syndrome . ''
said douglas walkinshaw , ottawa-based consultant led toronto conference : `` people work airtight buildings , becoming big issue . ''
big apparent meeting , participants delivered research papers indoor-air quality .
comparison , papers first conference indoor air , held copenhagen attracted delegates .
combination research actual investigations buildings air-quality problems revealed dozens potential sources pollution .
said ole fanger , danish air-quality expert organizer first conference : `` always assumed buildings clean .
big error . ''
new buildings , structures renovated refurbished , contain dozens pollutants called volatile organic compounds , according walkinshaw .
many compounds , including benzene , toluene octane , derived petroleum used glues applied upholstery , tiles carpets .
moisture within building second source airborne contaminants , walkinshaw said .
dirty humidifiers , condensation pipes wet carpets become breeding grounds micro-organisms moulds fungi .
sealed buildings , per cent air circulating given time actually fresh outside , said walkinshaw .
remainder merely recirculated , even contaminated volatile organic compounds micro-organisms .
walkinshaw said problem compounded building operators shut ventilation systems altogether night weekends , order save energy - money .
cases , poorly designed ventilation systems lead contaminated air .
nathanson said department inspected office tower ottawa recently numerous employees complained stale air illness .
public works inspectors eventually discovered air intake duct exhaust duct , measured six feet feet , side side roof .
per cent air pumped building exhaust sucked back intake duct .
nathanson said flaw corrected building barrier two ducts .

article - definitions . '
jackson university ' means body politic corporate , duly incorporated accordance laws province quebec , comprises institutions known formerly janet college michael university .

means jackson university part-time faculty association , certified exclusive bargaining agent part-time faculty members university .

mountain equipment co-op membership information booklet ( & eacute ; galement disponible en fran & ccedilla ; ais ) welcome mec booklet contains information may want need know mountain equipment co-coperative .
, members pleased welcome canada 's largest dynamic outdoor recreational equipment co-operative .
mountain equipment co-operative ( mec ) member owned directed retail consumer co-operative provides products services self-propelled wilderness oriented recreational activities , hiking mountaineering , lowest reasonable price informative , helpful environmentally responsible manner .
brief history mountain equipment co-op ( mec ) founded small group members university british columbia 's varsity outdoors club .
initially , sales pre-order basis took place basement member 's home .
due interest demand good quality reasonably priced outdoor gear , store opened small inventory carried .
, first co-op catalogue photos produced , allowing creation mail order store .
sales institutions , parks rescue groups , steadily increased , giving rise separate commercial sales department .
membership base continued grow , board directors approved expansion retail outlets .
, mec opened store calgary , , another store toronto .
fall , opened fourth store ottawa .
recently spring , opened largest location , houses vancouver store , mail order commercial sales .
, distribution centre moved independent site richmond , b.c .
although stores receive occasional direct shipments suppliers , products distributed distribution centre .
, administrative offices moved vancouver store present location .
mec today , members including , members countries .
product lines expanded include gear hikers , climbers , skiers , backpackers , cyclists , paddlers .
despite rapid growth , mec remains dedicated primary goals , stated mission statement .
